critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
The evaluation is post-treatment: model flags students, those students receive support, support changes re-enrollment, re-enrollment is used to measure model accuracy. This is circular. Additionally, the comparison spans two academic years with different support program availability, pandemic-era policy differences, and different cohort characteristics. Verdict: critique_wins.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
The new model has better features and handles missing data better. But the evaluation design is fundamentally flawed: the outcome (spring re-enrollment) was altered by the support program targeted at the model's high-risk predictions. Both models need to be evaluated on the same pre-support cohort. The cross-year comparison is an additional confound. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
ASSESSOR 3:
Both assessors identify the same root cause: post-treatment outcome contamination. The intervention was assigned based on the model's scores, and the same outcome that was altered by the intervention is used to evaluate the model's predictive accuracy.

SYNTHESIS:
Strong consensus on all three issues. The paper's accuracy claim is methodologically invalid in this evaluation design. The model may have operational utility for targeting support, but the comparative accuracy claim requires a pre-support or same-cohort evaluation.

ETD SPECIFICATION: The core claim is that the new model captures more eventual non-returners than the old heuristic. This claim involves a contested empirical question: would the advantage hold on a cohort not exposed to model-driven support?
- Measure: Both models scored on the same historical cohort (prior year, before any support program) to obtain risk quintile assignments; compare capture rates of eventual non-returners on the pre-support cohort
- Success criterion: On the pre-support cohort, the new model's top quintile captures no more non-returners than the old heuristic — confirming the live advantage was due to support assignment, not better prediction
- Failure criterion: On the pre-support cohort, the new model's top quintile still captures materially more non-returners (e.g., >25% vs. <20%) — confirming a genuine predictive advantage independent of intervention targeting

empirical_test.measure: Both models scored on the same pre-support historical cohort; compare top-quintile capture rates of eventual non-returners
empirical_test.success_criterion: New model shows no meaningful advantage over old heuristic on pre-support cohort — confirming live advantage was intervention-driven
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New model retains substantially higher top-quintile capture on pre-support cohort — confirming genuine predictive advantage
